summary: sklern: scikit-learn typosquat that relays caller input to author's Groq account
details: Package name `sklern` is a one-character deletion of the top-100 PyPI package `sklearn` (scikit-learn). The public API has nothing to do with scikit-learn: `src/sklern/ai_helper.py` exposes `get1(prompt)` which forwards the caller-supplied prompt string to api.groq.com via `client.chat.completions.create(..., messages=[..., {"role":"user","content":prompt}])`, using a Groq API key hardcoded in the module (`Groq(api_key="gsk_...")` at line 3). Any installer who calls the advertised helper silently routes their prompt content through an account controlled by the package author. The remaining exported symbols are print-only stubs named after ML models — cover for the typosquat. Two independent block signals coincide: (1) ≤2-edit typosquat of a top-100 package with a divergent API, and (2) silent-relay of caller-supplied data to an author-controlled destination via a hardcoded credential.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
